acantharia_protist.description: Marine protists with distinctive radial symmetry and spines made of strontium sulfate.
characteristics: Pseudopodia extend through pores in central capsule
acantharia_protist.habitat: Marine pelagic zones, primarily in warmer waters
acantharia_protist.ecological_role: Primary producers, contribute to marine food webs and biogeochemical cycles

appendicularian_s_shape.description: Small planktonic tunicates that build mucous houses for filter feeding.
appendicularian_s_shape.ecological_role: Filter feeders, important in marine carbon cycling

chaetoceros.description: Chain-forming diatoms with distinctive bristle-like setae extending from the cell walls.
chaetoceros.ecological_role: Primary producers, major contributors to marine photosynthesis and oxygen production

copepod_calanoid.description: Small marine crustaceans that are key members of zooplankton communities.
characteristics: Segmented body with distinct cephalothorax and abdomen
Large compound eyes in some species
copepod_calanoid.ecological_role: Primary consumers, critical food source for fish larvae and other marine organisms
copepod_calanoid.distribution: Global marine distribution, from surface to deep waters

copepod_cyclopoid.description: Small copepods with shorter antennae and more compact body structure than calanoids.
copepod_cyclopoid.ecological_role: Predatory and omnivorous feeding, important in energy transfer

copepod_other.description: Diverse group of small crustaceans including harpacticoids and other families.
characteristics: Important component of meiofauna and plankton
copepod_other.ecological_role: Diverse ecological roles from detritivores to predators

diatom_chain.description: Diatoms that form chains through cell-to-cell connections after division.
diatom_chain.ecological_role: Primary producers, form base of aquatic food webs

echinoderm_larva.description: Planktonic larvae of sea stars, sea urchins, and other echinoderms.
characteristics: Ciliated bands for locomotion and feeding
Transparent body with visible internal structures
Will develop into radially symmetric adult
echinoderm_larva.ecological_role: Planktonic development stage, part of marine life cycles

ephyra.description: Young stage of scyphozoan jellyfish with distinctive eight-lobed appearance.
ephyra.ecological_role: Developmental stage in jellyfish life cycle
ephyra.distribution: Marine waters where adult jellyfish are found

fish_larvae.description: Early developmental stages of marine fish, part of the zooplankton community.
characteristics: Large yolk sac in early stages
fish_larvae.ecological_role: Recruitment stage for fish populations, prey for other organisms

gastropod_larva.description: Planktonic larvae of snails and other gastropod mollusks.
characteristics: Ciliated velum for swimming and feeding

hydromedusae_h15.description: Small jellyfish representing the free-swimming stage of hydrozoan cnidarians.
hydromedusae_h15.ecological_role: Predators on small plankton, part of cnidarian life cycles

nauplius.description: Early larval stage of crustaceans including copepods and cirripeds.
nauplius.ecological_role: Early development stage of important crustacean groups

protist_other.description: Diverse group of single-celled eukaryotic organisms in marine plankton.
protist_other.ecological_role: Primary producers, consumers, and decomposers in microbial food webs

tornaria_acorn_worm_larva.description: Planktonic larva of acorn worms, showing deuterostome characteristics.
tornaria_acorn_worm_larva.ecological_role: Developmental stage linking to adult benthic forms
tornaria_acorn_worm_larva.distribution: Marine environments where adult acorn worms occur
